name: If This, Then That
concept: Conditionals — teach the robot to CHECK things and decide for itself

learn: So far the robot did the same thing every time. Now it can CHECK the world and decide!
The 🤔 if block asks a question: 👀 is the path ahead open? 💎 am I on a gem?
If the answer is YES, the robot does the ✅ blocks. The if-else block adds ❌ blocks for NO
This is how real programs make decisions — in JavaScript it's if (pathAhead()) { ... } else { ... }

blocks.name: if … do
blocks.text: checks the question — runs the blocks inside only when the answer is YES. Tap the question to change it!

blocks.name: if … do … else
blocks.text: YES runs the top blocks, NO runs the bottom blocks. The robot always picks one

blocks.name: path ahead?
blocks.text: YES when the square in front is open, NO when it's a wall

blocks.name: on gem?
blocks.text: YES when the robot is standing on a gem right now

intro.goal: Some gems are scattered where you least expect — let the robot CHECK instead of memorizing every step!

recap: Your robot now makes decisions: if the answer is YES do one thing, else do another
You combined loops + ifs: walk the whole path and grab a gem WHENEVER you're on one
One smart program with if-else can follow a whole winding path — no memorizing turns
In real JavaScript you wrote: if (pathAhead()) { moveForward(); } else { turnRight(); }

quiz.q: The robot runs ⬆️💎⬆️. When does it try to grab the gem?
quiz.why: Sequencing from World 1: blocks run in order — move, then collect, then move.

quiz.q: 🔁 repeat 5 times [⬆️] — how many squares does the robot walk?
quiz.why: Loops from World 2: the move runs 5 times.

quiz.q: Which program is SHORTER but does the same as ⬆️⬆️⬆️⬆️⬆️⬆️⬆️⬆️?
quiz.why: A loop repeats the move 8 times using just 2 blocks.

quiz.q: 🤔 if 💎 on gem? ✅ collect — and the robot is NOT on a gem. What happens?
quiz.why: When the answer is NO, the blocks inside the if are simply skipped.

quiz.q: What kind of thing is 👀 path ahead?
choices: ❓ a question the robot answers YES or NO
quiz.why: Sensors are questions. The if block uses the answer to decide.

quiz.q: 🤔 if 👀 path ahead? ✅ move ❌ turn right — the robot is facing a WALL. What does it do?
quiz.why: Path ahead is NO, so the else (❌) blocks run: turn right.

quiz.q: Gems are scattered on random squares along a long path. What's the SMART plan?
choices: 🔁 loop: move, and if on gem — collect
quiz.why: Loops + ifs handle ANY gem layout — that's why programmers love them.

quiz.q: The robot faces a wall and runs: 🤔 if 👀 path ahead? ✅ move ❌ collect. Which block runs?
quiz.why: The if picks exactly ONE branch — the answer was NO, so the else runs.

levels.name: Gem or Not?
levels.hint: NEW! 🤔 if block. Try: repeat 6 [ move, if 💎 on gem? ✅ collect ] — the robot checks every square!

levels.name: Mystery Gems
levels.hint: Same trick, longer hallway: walk 8 and CHECK each square for a gem.

levels.name: Follow the Bend
levels.hint: NEW! if-else: if 👀 path ahead? ✅ move ❌ turn right. The robot finds the bend by itself!

levels.name: The Long Way Around
levels.hint: Two bends this time — but the SAME if-else handles both. Just repeat it enough times!

levels.name: Bend + Gems
levels.hint: Combine BOTH checks in one loop: if path — move, else turn. Then: if on gem — collect.

levels.name: Lefty Bends
levels.hint: This path bends the OTHER way — when there's no path ahead, turn LEFT.

levels.name: Mystery Maze
levels.hint: One loop, two questions each time: can I move? am I on a gem? The robot handles the rest.

levels.name: Check to the Flag
levels.hint: Gems AND a flag 🏁. Your checking loop ends right on the flag — count your repeats!

levels.name: Double Trouble
levels.hint: Left bends and three sneaky gems. Same smart loop — check path, check gem, every time.

levels.name: Boss: Sensor Master
levels.hint: Boss! The longest path yet — 3 hidden gems and the flag. One clever checking loop does it ALL.
